metadata.summary: The dispute centers on a 2.3-acre agricultural plot in Kharagpur, originally owned by the late Shri Anil Chatterjee, who died in 2018. His two daughters, Priya Chatterjee and Sunita Roy, now contest ownership, with Priya claiming sole inheritance under a 2017 will, while Sunita asserts the will is forged and that her father verbally bequeathed the land equally before his death. The property has been under dispute since 2020, when Priya registered a mutation in her name using a notarized will and a forged signature on the 2016 land revenue record. Sunita, who lived with her father and managed the farm, alleges her sister bribed the local revenue clerk, Mr. Debashish Mukherjee, to alter records and falsify witness affidavits. Complicating matters, a third party, Dr. Arjun Mehta — a distant cousin and retired orthopedic surgeon — claims he was named as a trustee in a 2015 handwritten document, which Priya claims is a draft with no legal standing. The case has attracted media attention due to allegations of a land mafia connection and the sudden death of the original revenue officer, Mr. Ramesh Das, who was scheduled to testify. Forensic analysis of the will’s ink and paper shows inconsistencies, but the notary who certified it has vanished. The land’s boundary markers have been moved twice since 2021, and satellite imagery from 2020 contradicts the survey submitted by Priya’s legal team.

hidden_ground_truth.true_motive: To sell the land to a real estate developer for ₹4.2 crore, as Priya was deeply in debt from failed business ventures and needed to cover embezzlement from her father’s savings.
true_timeline: 2015: Anil Chatterjee drafts a handwritten note naming Dr. Arjun Mehta as trustee for equal division among daughters, but never signs it.
2017: Priya forges a will with her father’s signature using a template she found in his study, with help from Nandini Sen, a typist who knew his handwriting.
2018: Anil Chatterjee dies of cardiac arrest; Sunita is unaware of the forged will.
2019: Priya bribes Debashish Mukherjee to alter the 2016 land revenue record to show her as sole heir, backdating the entry.
2020: Priya files mutation application using the forged will and altered record; Sunita files objection.
2021: Ramesh Das, the revenue officer who reviewed the mutation, is found dead in his home — ruled suicide, but evidence suggests poisoning.
2022: Nandini Sen, fearing exposure, flees to Nepal after receiving a threat from Priya.
2023: Priya hires a local contractor to move boundary stones to claim an additional 0.4 acres, triggering the current legal case.
forged_evidence: The 2017 will signed by Anil Chatterjee — ink matches a pen purchased by Priya in 2017, but the paper is from a 2015 batch found in Anil’s drawer; signature is traced.
The 2016 land revenue record entry — digitally altered in the Kharagpur Tehsil database; original paper copy was destroyed.
The notarization certificate — notarized by a fake notary whose seal was forged using a stolen stamp from a defunct law firm.

injected_contradictions.description: Priya claims the will was signed on 12/03/2017, but the notary’s logbook shows no appointment that day — yet the notary’s signature is present.

Sunita says her father never trusted banks, yet bank records show a ₹5 lakh withdrawal from his account on 11/03/2017 — the day before the will was allegedly signed — which Priya claims was for legal fees.
Dr. Mehta says he was given the 2015 handwritten note as a 'draft for future use,' but the paper is from a notebook purchased by Sunita, not Anil.
Mrs. Ghosh testifies she saw Anil sign the will in 2017, but her own diary entry from March 2017 says she was hospitalized for pneumonia from 08/03/2017 to 20/03/2017.
The boundary survey submitted by Priya shows a 2020 measurement, but the GPS coordinates match a 2018 survey that was never officially filed.
Nandini Sen’s affidavit claims she typed the will on 10/03/2017, but her employer’s attendance log shows she was on leave that week.

contradictions.claim_a: Priya claims the will was signed on 12/03/2017, and the notary certified it the same day.
contradictions.claim_b: The notary’s official logbook shows no appointment on 12/03/2017, and the notary’s signature matches a stamp stolen from a firm that closed in 2016.

contradictions.claim_a: Mrs. Anjali Ghosh testifies she witnessed Anil Chatterjee signing the will on 12/03/2017.
contradictions.claim_b: Mrs. Ghosh’s personal diary confirms she was hospitalized for pneumonia from 08/03/2017 to 20/03/2017, making her presence impossible.

contradictions.claim_a: The 2017 will is printed on paper from a 2015 batch found in Anil Chatterjee’s study.
contradictions.claim_b: The ink used is from a Parker pen purchased by Priya on 05/03/2017, and the signature shows signs of tracing using a lightbox.

contradictions.claim_a: Priya claims the ₹5 lakh withdrawal from Anil’s account on 11/03/2017 was for legal fees to draft the will.
contradictions.claim_b: The bank’s transaction code shows the withdrawal was made as a cash transfer to an unregistered account under the name 'P. C. Enterprises' — a shell company Priya created in 2016.

contradictions.claim_a: Dr. Arjun Mehta claims he was named trustee in a 2015 handwritten note and has held it since.
contradictions.claim_b: The notebook the note was written in was purchased by Sunita in January 2015, and the handwriting matches Anil’s, but the paper is from a different batch than Anil’s known stationery.

contradictions.claim_a: The 2016 land revenue record shows Priya as sole heir, certified by Debashish Mukherjee.
contradictions.claim_b: The original paper copy of the 2016 record was destroyed in a fire in 2019, but the digital version shows metadata indicating edits made on 04/04/2019 — after Anil’s death and before the mutation filing.

contradictions.claim_a: Nandini Sen claims she typed the will on 10/03/2017 at her workplace.
contradictions.claim_b: Her employer’s attendance records show she was on approved leave from 08/03/2017 to 14/03/2017 due to family emergency.

contradictions.claim_a: Priya’s survey shows the boundary extends 15 meters east, claiming additional land.
contradictions.claim_b: Satellite imagery from 2020 shows the boundary was identical to the 2018 survey, and the current eastern marker was installed in January 2023.

contradictions.claim_a: Dr. Mehta says he was never involved in the property and only received the note as a 'courtesy.'
contradictions.claim_b: A 2016 WhatsApp message from Priya to Dr. Mehta says, 'You’re the only one who can keep this safe — don’t let Sunita get her hands on it.'

contradictions.claim_a: Priya claims she inherited her father’s savings and used them for medical care.
contradictions.claim_b: Anil’s bank statements show ₹12 lakh was transferred to Priya’s account in 2017, but ₹8.5 lakh was transferred to a foreign account in Dubai under the name 'P. Chatterjee' in 2021 — matching her passport details.

documents.content: I, Sunita Roy, daughter of the late Shri Anil Chatterjee, residing at Village Kharagpur, P.O. Kharagpur, Dist. Paschim Medinipur, West Bengal, hereby file this FIR regarding fraudulent acquisition of ancestral agricultural land. My father, Shri Anil Chatterjee, passed away on 12/06/2018. He was the sole owner of a 2.3-acre agricultural plot in Kharagpur, which I have managed since 2005 alongside him. In 2020, my sister, Priya Chatterjee, filed a mutation application in the Kharagpur Tehsil Office claiming sole ownership based on a 2017 will. I was unaware of this will until I received notice of the mutation. Upon investigation, I discovered that the 2017 will is forged: the signature is traced, the paper is from a 2015 batch found in my father’s study, and the notary who certified it has vanished. Further, the 2016 land revenue record was digitally altered on 04/04/2019 — after my father’s death — by Revenue Clerk Debashish Mukherjee, whom I believe was bribed. A neighbor, Mrs. Anjali Ghosh, falsely testified she witnessed the signing of the will on 12/03/2017, but hospital records prove she was hospitalized from 08/03/2017 to 20/03/2017. The original revenue officer, Mr. Ramesh Das, who reviewed the mutation, was found dead in 2021 — ruled suicide, but signs of poisoning were present. Boundary stones were moved in January 2023 to claim an additional 0.4 acres, confirmed by satellite imagery from 2020. I request immediate investigation into forgery, criminal breach of trust, corruption, and possible murder. I also request protection, as I have received threats from Priya’s associates.

My father, Shri Anil Chatterjee, was in poor health since 2016. On 12/03/2017, he called me, my sister Sunita, and our neighbor Mrs. Anjali Ghosh to his residence. He declared he wished to leave the entire property to me, as Sunita had shown no interest in farming and I had taken care of his medical expenses. He signed the will in front of us all. The document was notarized the same day by Notary P. K. Banerjee, whose seal is on the document. I withdrew ₹5 lakh from my father’s account on 11/03/2017 to pay legal fees for drafting the will. The land revenue record was updated in 2016 because my father had already decided to make me the sole heir. I never had any motive to forge anything. I was deeply attached to my father and cared for him until his death. I have no idea why Sunita is accusing me. As for the boundary stones — they were placed according to the original survey. The satellite images she refers to are outdated. I did not bribe anyone. I am the rightful heir.

I lived with my father from 2005 until his death in 2018. He always said he would divide the land equally between my sister and me. He never mentioned a will. In early 2017, he told me he was drafting a note with Dr. Arjun Mehta as trustee to ensure fairness. I found that handwritten note in his drawer after his death — it was on my own notebook, which I bought in January 2015. I never saw any signed will. When Priya filed the mutation in 2020, I was shocked. I checked the revenue records — the 2016 entry showing me as non-heir was clearly altered. The notary’s seal is fake; the firm closed in 2016. Mrs. Ghosh couldn’t have witnessed the signing — she was in the hospital. The ₹5 lakh withdrawal was not for legal fees — it went to a shell company Priya created. I have bank statements. The boundary stones were moved in January 2023 — I have photos. Mr. Ramesh Das, the officer who reviewed the mutation, was found dead with a note in his pocket that said, 'They know I know.' I believe he was poisoned. Priya is in debt. She was embezzling from my father’s savings. She hired Nandini Sen to type the will — but Nandini was on leave that week. I have her employer’s records. Priya is a criminal.

I served as Revenue Clerk from 2010 to 2020. The 2016 land revenue record was updated in 2016 as per the request of Shri Anil Chatterjee. He submitted a handwritten note stating he wished to transfer the entire property to his daughter Priya. I verified the document and updated the record accordingly. I have no knowledge of any forgery. The digital records were maintained properly. I retired in 2020. I do not know why anyone would accuse me. I have no connection to any bribes. I am a man of integrity. I was never threatened. I have no idea about the death of Mr. Ramesh Das.

metadata.relation_to_case: Former Typist, Claimed to Have Typed the Will
metadata.occupation: Typist (Currently Absent, Reported to Have Fled to Nepal)

documents.content: I worked at Shree Vani Typing Center from 2014 to 2021. I was hired by a woman named Priya in March 2017 to type a document for her father. I typed a single-page will on 10/03/2017. I did not read its contents. She gave me a handwritten draft and asked me to make it formal. I signed an affidavit to that effect. I was not paid much. I do not know if the signature was real. I was on leave from 08/03/2017 to 14/03/2017 due to my mother’s illness — I submitted a leave form. But I did go to work on 10/03/2017 because my sister was hospitalized and I had to deliver the document. I don’t know why my attendance log says I was absent. I have no idea about the notary or the land records. I left India because I feared for my life after receiving a call from an unknown number saying, 'You’re the only loose end.'

metadata.relation_to_case: Distant Cousin, Claimed Trustee in 2015 Note

documents.content: I am a distant cousin of the late Anil Chatterjee. In 2015, he gave me a handwritten note on a notebook page, asking me to act as trustee to ensure his daughters received equal shares of the land. He said he was drafting a formal will but wanted a safekeeping. I kept it in my personal files. I never signed it. I never acted on it. I have no knowledge of the 2017 will. I never received any communication from Priya about the property. I do not know why Sunita says the notebook belongs to her — I have the original page. I only saw the note once. I have no financial interest in this land. I am merely a custodian of a family document.

I knew Anil Chatterjee for over 30 years. He was a kind man. On the morning of 12/03/2017, I was at his house. He called me in to witness him sign a will leaving the land to Priya. I saw him sign it. I signed as a witness. I remember the day because it was a sunny Tuesday, and I had just finished my morning walk. I have no reason to lie. I was not in the hospital then. I don’t know why my diary says otherwise — I must have written it wrong. I am 72 years old. I cannot remember every date. But I know what I saw.

This is a reconstructed statement from the personal diary of Ramesh Das, recovered from his residence after his death on 18/07/2021. Entry dated 15/04/2020: 'Mutation application filed by Priya Chatterjee on forged will. Paper from 2015 batch. Ink from Parker pen bought in March 2017. Signature traced. Notary seal is fake — firm closed in 2016. Debashish Mukherjee altered 2016 record — digital trace shows edit on 04/04/2019. Nandini Sen was on leave. Mrs. Ghosh hospitalized. Bank withdrawal to shell company. This is fraud. I will report to CBI tomorrow. If anything happens to me — it’s Priya.'

Examination of the 2017 Will (E1): 1. Paper: Spectral analysis confirms the paper is from a batch manufactured in January 2015, consistent with stationery found in Anil Chatterjee’s study. 2. Ink: Chromatography matches Parker Pen Model 51, purchased by Priya Chatterjee on 05/03/2017 from a local stationery shop in Kharagpur (receipt attached). 3. Signature: Digital enhancement reveals tracing patterns consistent with use of a lightbox — the signature is a trace of a prior genuine signature, not an original. 4. Notary Seal: The seal impression matches a stolen stamp from 'Roy & Associates,' a law firm that ceased operations in 2016. The notary's signature is a photocopied forgery. Conclusion: The 2017 will is a forged document, created after 05/03/2017 using paper from 2015 and ink from a pen purchased by Priya Chatterjee.

metadata.issued_by: National Cyber Forensics Unit, New Delhi

documents.content: Examination of the Kharagpur Tehsil Digital Land Record (2016 Entry): 1. Metadata of the digital file shows the last edit occurred on 04/04/2019 at 14:23 IST. 2. IP address traced to a terminal in the Tehsil Office, logged in with Debashish Mukherjee’s credentials. 3. The original paper copy was reported destroyed in a 2019 fire — but the digital file was not backed up until after the fire, indicating premeditated deletion. 4. No authorized access log exists for the date of alteration. Conclusion: The 2016 land revenue record was digitally altered posthumously by Debashish Mukherjee to support Priya Chatterjee’s mutation claim.

metadata.issued_by: State Bank of India, Kharagpur Branch

documents.content: Transaction History of Account No. SBIN/123456789 (Anil Chatterjee): - 11/03/2017: ₹5,00,000 withdrawn as cash transfer to 'P. C. Enterprises' (Account No. SBIN/987654321). Transaction Code: 'CT-UNREG' (Unregistered Entity). - 15/03/2017: ₹12,00,000 transferred from Anil’s account to Priya Chatterjee’s personal account. - 12/08/2021: ₹8,50,000 transferred from Priya’s account to Dubai-based account 'P. Chatterjee' (SWIFT: DUBAI123). The shell company 'P. C. Enterprises' was registered on 02/06/2016 with a fake PAN. Priya Chatterjee is the sole director. The ₹5 lakh withdrawal was not for legal fees — no legal invoice or receipt exists. The transfer to Dubai matches Priya’s passport details.

metadata.issued_by: Kharagpur Tehsil Office, Land Records Division

documents.content: Land Record No. KHR/AGRI/2018/0072: Plot No. 12B, Village Kharagpur, 2.3 acres. Original owner: Anil Chatterjee. Mutation Application (2020): Filed by Priya Chatterjee, claiming sole inheritance under 2017 will. Original 2016 record (paper copy) was destroyed in fire on 15/02/2019. Digital record updated on 04/04/2019 to reflect Priya as sole heir. Survey Map submitted by Priya (2020) shows boundary extending 15m east. However, satellite imagery from 2018 (Google Earth Archive) shows no such extension. Boundary stone at eastern edge installed on 15/01/2023, as per contractor’s invoice (attached). No official survey was filed in 2020. The 2020 survey coordinates match the 2018 survey — indicating fabrication.

Autopsy Report: Deceased: Ramesh Das, Male, 58. Cause of Death: Cardiac arrest. Toxicology Report: Presence of lethal dose of curare alkaloid (natural poison) in bloodstream. No prior medical history of cardiac condition. No signs of struggle. Note found in pocket: 'They know I know.' Death ruled suicide by local police, but forensic evidence indicates poisoning. Time of death: Between 22:00 and 00:00 on 17/07/2021. No fingerprints on poison container. Poison likely administered via food or drink. No suspects identified yet. Case referred to CBI.

WhatsApp Message Chain: Priya Chatterjee (9876543210) to Dr. Arjun Mehta (9988776655), dated 14/06/2016: 'You’re the only one who can keep this safe — don’t let Sunita get her hands on it.' Attached: Image of the 2015 handwritten note. Message deleted from Priya’s phone but recovered from cloud backup. Dr. Mehta claims he received it as a 'courtesy' — but the message shows intent to conceal the note from Sunita. No prior communication between them on property matters before this date. Message sent from Priya’s home IP address.

CCTV footage from Anil Chatterjee’s residence (2017): No cameras were installed inside the house. External camera at gate shows Priya entering on 12/03/2017 at 10:15 AM. Mrs. Anjali Ghosh does not appear on camera that day. Footage shows Mrs. Ghosh entering her own house at 10:30 AM on 15/03/2017 — confirming she was hospitalized on 12/03/2017. Boundary stone installation on 15/01/2023: Footage from nearby shop shows Priya’s hired contractor, Raju Singh, moving two stones with a tractor at 8:00 AM. Priya is visible at the site at 8:45 AM, handing him cash. No official surveyor present.

Investigation Summary: This case involves a complex property fraud centered on forgery, corruption, and possible homicide. Forensic evidence confirms the 2017 will is forged. The 2016 land record was altered by Debashish Mukherjee. Nandini Sen was on leave when the will was typed. Mrs. Ghosh was hospitalized and could not have witnessed the signing. Dr. Mehta’s involvement is passive — he was used to lend credibility. Financial trail shows Priya Chatterjee embezzled ₹12 lakh from her father’s account and transferred ₹8.5 lakh to Dubai. The death of Ramesh Das was homicide by curare poisoning. Boundary stones were illegally moved by Priya’s contractor. Evidence points to Priya Chatterjee as the principal architect of the fraud. Accomplices: Debashish Mukherjee (bribery), Nandini Sen (forged affidavit), and possibly Nandini Sen’s employer who falsified attendance records. Motive: Debt and desire to sell land to real estate developer for ₹4.2 crore. Recommendation: Charge sheet under IPC Sections 420 (cheating), 467 (forgery of valuable security), 468 (forgery for purpose of cheating), 302 (murder), 120B (criminal conspiracy), and Prevention of Corruption Act. Arrest warrants issued for Priya Chatterjee, Debashish Mukherjee. Nandini Sen is absconding. Dr. Mehta and Mrs. Ghosh are not criminally liable but their testimonies are unreliable.

IN THE COURT OF THE SESSIONS JUDGE, PASCHIM MEDINIPUR

CHARGE SHEET UNDER SECTION 173 CrPC

Accused: 1. Priya Chatterjee, 2. Debashish Mukherjee, 3. Nandini Sen (absconding)

Offences: 1. IPC 420 - Cheating 2. IPC 467 - Forgery of Valuable Security 3. IPC 468 - Forgery for Purpose of Cheating 4. IPC 120B - Criminal Conspiracy 5. IPC 302 - Murder (of Ramesh Das) 6. Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 - Section 7 (taking gratification)

Facts: The accused, Priya Chatterjee, forged a will dated 12/03/2017, using a Parker pen purchased by her on 05/03/2017 and paper from her father’s 2015 stationery. The signature was traced. She bribed Debashish Mukherjee to alter the 2016 land record on 04/04/2019. She hired Nandini Sen to type the will while she was on leave, and obtained a false affidavit. She transferred ₹12 lakh from her father’s account to her own and ₹8.5 lakh to Dubai. She moved boundary stones illegally. Ramesh Das, who discovered the fraud, was poisoned. Evidence: Forensic report, digital forensics, financial records, CCTV, WhatsApp message, autopsy report, hospital records, attendance logs. The accused are jointly liable for criminal conspiracy and murder.

JUDGE: The court has reviewed the forensic reports, digital evidence, and witness testimonies. The will is forged. The land record altered. The witness testimony of Mrs. Ghosh is discredited. The death of Ramesh Das is under murder investigation. The accused, Priya Chatterjee, is present. The prosecution has submitted the charge sheet. Defense counsel, Mr. Sen Gupta, argues that no direct evidence ties Priya to the poisoning. The court notes: the forged documents, the financial trail, the boundary tampering, and the motive are overwhelming. The defense has not produced any credible evidence to refute the forensic findings. The court declares Priya Chatterjee as the principal offender. Debashish Mukherjee is charged with corruption. Nandini Sen is declared absconding. The court orders arrest warrants for all accused. Hearing adjourned until 05/09/2023 for framing of charges.

queries.question: Which piece of evidence is most critical in proving the will is forged?
How do the financial records contradict Priya’s claim about the ₹5 lakh withdrawal?
Why is Mrs. Anjali Ghosh’s testimony unreliable?
What is the motive behind the crime?
Reconstruct the timeline of the forgery and fraud.
Who are the accomplices, and what roles did they play?
Is Dr. Arjun Mehta complicit in the fraud?
What forensic evidence links Priya to the ink used in the forged will?
What is the likelihood of conviction for murder of Ramesh Das based on current evidence?

expected_answers.answer: Priya Chatterjee is the actual perpetrator. She forged the 2017 will using her father’s paper and a pen she purchased, traced his signature, bribed Debashish Mukherjee to alter land records, hired Nandini Sen to falsely attest to typing the will, and orchestrated the murder of Ramesh Das to prevent exposure. Her motive was financial desperation — she needed ₹4.2 crore to cover debts and embezzlement from her father’s savings.

The most critical evidence is the forensic report linking the ink in the will to a Parker pen purchased by Priya on 05/03/2017, while the paper is from a 2015 batch found in her father’s study. This proves the document was created after the paper was already in the house, contradicting the claim it was signed on 12/03/2017. Combined with the traced signature, this is irrefutable proof of forgery.

Priya claims the ₹5 lakh withdrawal on 11/03/2017 was for legal fees to draft the will. However, bank records show the funds were transferred to 'P. C. Enterprises,' a shell company Priya created in 2016. No legal invoice, receipt, or law firm exists to support the claim. The transaction code 'CT-UNREG' confirms it was a non-legal, unregistered transfer — likely for personal use or to fund the forgery operation.

Mrs. Anjali Ghosh’s testimony is unreliable because her personal diary confirms she was hospitalized for pneumonia from 08/03/2017 to 20/03/2017, making it impossible for her to have witnessed the signing on 12/03/2017. CCTV footage from her home also shows her arriving on 15/03/2017, further disproving her presence on the alleged signing date. Her claim of memory error is implausible given the specificity of her testimony.

The motive is financial desperation. Priya had failed business ventures and was deeply in debt. She had already embezzled ₹12 lakh from her father’s savings. She planned to sell the 2.3-acre land to a real estate developer for ₹4.2 crore to cover her debts and conceal her theft. The forged will and altered records were tools to gain sole ownership to enable the sale.

Timeline: 2015 — Anil drafts a handwritten note naming Dr. Mehta as trustee (on Sunita’s notebook). 05/03/2017 — Priya buys Parker pen. 11/03/2017 — ₹5 lakh withdrawn to shell company. 12/03/2017 — Priya forges will using the pen and 2015 paper. 04/04/2019 — Debashish alters land record digitally. 12/06/2018 — Anil dies. 2020 — Priya files mutation. 2021 — Ramesh Das discovers fraud, is poisoned. 2022 — Nandini Sen flees to Nepal after threats. 01/2023 — Boundary stones moved to claim extra land. 05/2023 — Sunita files FIR.

Accomplices: Debashish Mukherjee — bribed to alter the 2016 land record digitally; Nandini Sen — hired to type the will and sign a false affidavit, despite being on leave. Both acted under Priya’s direction. Nandini Sen was threatened into silence before fleeing. Dr. Mehta was manipulated into holding the 2015 note to lend credibility to the claim of equal division, but he is not criminally liable.

Dr. Arjun Mehta is not complicit. He received the 2015 handwritten note as a 'courtesy' from Anil, and had no knowledge of the 2017 will. However, Priya’s WhatsApp message shows she used him to keep the note away from Sunita, indicating manipulation. He is an unwitting tool, not a co-conspirator. His testimony is truthful but incomplete — he did not realize the note’s potential to be used in a fraud.

The forensic report from CFSL, Kolkata, confirms that the ink in the forged will matches the chemical composition of a Parker Pen Model 51 purchased by Priya on 05/03/2017 from a local shop in Kharagpur. The receipt and purchase record were recovered from her bank statement. This proves the will was written after that date, contradicting the claim it was signed on 12/03/2017 with her father’s pen.

The likelihood of conviction for murder is high. The toxicology report confirms curare poisoning, a rare and deliberate method. Ramesh Das’s diary explicitly named Priya as the threat. Priya had motive (to silence him), means (access to poison via her network), and opportunity (he was alone at home). While no direct eyewitness, circumstantial evidence is overwhelming under Indian law. The charge sheet includes murder under Section 302, and the court has already noted the strength of the evidence.
